message:
perf(core): skip memdb rebuild when schema cookie unchanged on strict-no-fallback reload (OPT-B2)

perf_update_delete flamegraph showed reload_memdb_from_txn_with_mode at 1.13%
self-time on the UPDATE/DELETE workload. Every commit advances
visible_commit_seq, which in turn trips refresh_memdb_if_stale and drives
the full reload — including a sqlite_master B-tree scan that rebuilds schema
metadata identical to what's already cached whenever no DDL has run.

Fast path: when the page-1 schema cookie matches the connection's cached
schema cookie AND the caller did not request row hydration, the sqlite_master
scan produces an image byte-identical to the one already in self.schema/
self.db. Skip the scan, bump memdb_visible_commit_seq + change_counter, and
return.

Guarded by:
- `!hydrate_rows` — eagerly-hydrated memdb paths still need row refresh
  because the cookie doesn't track row inserts.
- `schema_cookie != 0` — the zero-header "empty database" path is handled
  by the existing early-return above.
- `schema_cookie == *self.schema_cookie.borrow()` — any DDL increments
  this and forces the full rebuild.
- `!memdb_rows_loaded` — paired invariant with the strict-no-fallback flow
  that this fast path targets.
- `!memdb_requires_active_txn_reload` — the forced-reload bit bypasses the
  cookie shortcut so explicit invalidations always redraw.
- pending_memdb_direct_upserts empty and no pending vtab preserve — we
  can't drop those by skipping the rebuild.

Impact (perf_update_delete 10k/10/both, 3 runs):
  baseline   update=74-77 ms, delete=43-47 ms, per-row-update 7421-7766 ns
  post-B2    update=72-76 ms, delete=42    ms, per-row-update 7220-7618 ns
  ~2-4% update improvement, ~5-7% delete improvement within run-to-run noise.

Verified:
- self_connection_read_your_own_write + peer_races tests pass.
- Schema/CREATE TABLE/ALTER TABLE/concurrent test suites: same 7 pre-
  existing failures on both baseline and with-fix (no regression).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
